controlnote.p: This collection contains 16 published digital objects.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday October 10, 2025 at 20:01

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: New Haven Musicians is a growing collection of audio interviews with singers, songwriters and players from the New Haven area who perform in clubs, bars, coffeehouses, and other venues in the area. Covering the 1960s to the present day, the collection features artists from across the New Haven musical community, representing a wide variety of genre and cultures that continue to impact the local music scene.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research. The finding aid includes links to stream most interviews online Yale University Library's AV access system, Aviary. Yale users can login to Aviary using their Yale University ID, to stream these interviews without restriction. Other users are able to request access to interviews directly through Aviary. For detailed access instructions, see OHAM's website.
Please submit a Transcript Request to inquire about the availability of a transcript.
Some interviews may be restricted. Access to these restricted interviews may only be granted to a patron who has first obtained direct permission from the interviewee or their estate.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Rights and permissions for Oral History of American Music materials vary between individual interviews. Researchers wishing to publish or broadcast Oral History of American Music materials are responsible for obtaining direct authorization from copyright holders: generally, the interviewees or their estates.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: NHM, New Haven Musicians, Oral History of American Music, in the Music Library of Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged alphabetically by surname of interviewee.
